core_mechanism: Zeno-type suppression of a diffusion process by frequent stochastic dephasing — increasing the rate of random measurement-like perturbations paradoxically slows the diffusive spreading of a collective phase variable, with a size-dependent amplification factor (here of order N/log N) enhancing the suppression
brief_reason: The abstract states that noise-induced dephasing slows collisional phase-diffusion in the quantum Zeno limit and that the degree of suppression grows by a bosonic factor of order N/log N with particle number, i.e. frequent stochastic perturbation freezing a diffusive process with a scale-dependent enhancement.
